question: Which MIME header is the opt-in flag that tells a user agent to switch into MIME mode?
explanation: MIME-Version: 1.0 is the version tag. A user agent that sees it switches into MIME mode; a message without it is assumed to be plain RFC 5322 ASCII.

question: Which Content-Transfer-Encoding produces safe 7-bit ASCII output for arbitrary binary data?
explanation: Base64 (RFC 4648 §4) packs 6 bits per character into the 7-bit-safe A-Za-z0-9+/ alphabet. It is the standard way to ship arbitrary binary through a 7-bit-clean transport. quoted-printable is more compact for mostly-ASCII text but expands binary data.

question: A multipart/alternative message has three parts in order: text/plain, text/html, application/pdf. According to RFC 2046, what does this ordering tell the user agent?
options: A. The parts are equal options; the user must pick one
B. The parts should be displayed from least to most expressive so a non-MIME UA can still read the first
C. The parts should be displayed from most to least expressive so a modern UA can show the best first
D. The order is irrelevant for multipart/alternative
explanation: RFC 2046 §5.1.4 specifies that multipart/alternative parts should be ordered from least faithful / most universal to most faithful / least universal, so that a pre-MIME user agent still sees the text/plain version.
